customNotes.content:
LauncherHub Proxy Analysis - Complete API Specification

## What is LauncherHub?
LauncherHub is a firmware distribution and OTA (over-the-air) update system for ESP32 and other embedded devices. The proxy (launcherhub-proxy) at https://github.com/skrashevich/launcherhub-proxy aggregates firmware from multiple repositories and provides a standardized API for firmware clients.

Upstream: https://mrekin.duckdns.org/flasher/api (official LauncherHub API)

## Architecture
- Python FastAPI service on port 9945
- Acts as transparent proxy + device family aggregator
- In-memory caching of sources and device lists
- Concurrent upstream requests (asyncio)
- Partition table parsing from ESP32 binaries

## Three Main Endpoints

### 1. GET /firmwares
**Two modes:**

**Mode A - Firmware List (Family Query)**
- Query: `category=<device_name>` [+optional filters]
- Optional params: `order_by` (name|downloads|date), `page` (1-indexed), `q` (search), `star` (1=starred only)
- Returns paginated list across device family
- Response: `{total, page_size, page, items: [{fid, name, author, star}]}`
- Example: `GET /firmwares?category=t-deck&page=1`

**Mode B - Firmware Detail (Single Version)**
- Query: `fid=<device>|<version>[|<src>]`
- Returns full firmware object with partition metadata
- Response: `{fid, name, author, star, versions: [{version, published_at, file, zip_url, Fs, partition fields...}]}`
- Example: `GET /firmwares?fid=t-deck|v2.7.18.fb3bf78|Official%20repo`

### 2. GET /download
- Binary streaming endpoint
- Params: `fid=<device>|<version>|<src>&file=<url>` (file is fallback)
- Response: HTTP 200 + binary stream (application/octet-stream)
- Headers: Content-Length, Content-Disposition (firmware-{device}-{version}.bin)
- Example: `GET /download?fid=t-deck|v2.7.18.fb3bf78|Official%20repo`

## Data Formats

### FID (Firmware ID)
Format: `<device>|<version>|<src>`
- device: e.g. "t-deck", "t-deck-tft", "t-deck-plus"
- version: e.g. "v2.7.18.fb3bf78"
- src: e.g. "Official repo", "svk" (URL-encoded if spaces; defaults to "Official repo")
- URL encoding: src="Official repo" → "Official%20repo" in the fid
- Example: `t-deck|v2.7.18.fb3bf78|Official%20repo`

### Firmware List Response
```json
{
  "total": 45,
  "page_size": 10,
  "page": 1,
  "items": [
    {
      "fid": "t-deck|v2.7.19.bb3d6d5|Official%20repo",
      "name": "t-deck v2.7.19.bb3d6d5 [Official repo]",
      "author": "Official repo",
      "star": false
    }
  ]
}
```

### Firmware Detail Response (Version Object)
```json
{
  "fid": "t-deck|v2.7.19.bb3d6d5|Official%20repo",
  "name": "t-deck v2.7.19.bb3d6d5 [Official repo]",
  "author": "Official repo",
  "star": false,
  "versions": [
    {
      "version": "v2.7.19.bb3d6d5",
      "published_at": "2024-03-14T10:30:00Z",
      "file": "https://mrekin.duckdns.org/flasher/api/firmware?t=t-deck&v=v2.7.19.bb3d6d5&u=1&p=fw&e=true&src=Official%20repo",
      "zip_url": "https://mrekin.duckdns.org/flasher/api/firmware?t=t-deck&v=v2.7.19.bb3d6d5&u=5&p=fw&e=true&src=Official%20repo",
      "Fs": 3582768,
      "nb": false,
      "s": true,
      "f": false,
      "f2": false,
      "as": 2097152,
      "ao": 65536,
      "ss": 1048576,
      "so": 3145728,
      "fs": 0,
      "fo": 0,
      "fs2": 0,
      "fo2": 0
    }
  ]
}
```

## Partition Table Fields (ESP32)
- `nb`: boolean - no-bootloader flag (True=app-only, no PT; False=has partition table)
- `s`: boolean - has SPIFFS/LittleFS
- `f`: boolean - has FAT partition 1
- `f2`: boolean - has FAT partition 2
- `ao`: integer - app offset (usually 0x10000)
- `as`: integer - app size (bytes)
- `so`: integer - SPIFFS offset
- `ss`: integer - SPIFFS size
- `fo`: integer - FAT1 offset
- `fs`: integer - FAT1 size
- `fo2`: integer - FAT2 offset
- `fs2`: integer - FAT2 size
- `Fs`: integer - total file size (binary)

## Device Family Matching
Algorithm: "longest dash-separated prefix"
1. If category exact match in known devices → use it
2. Otherwise, try progressively shorter prefixes (left-to-right)
3. Return longest matching prefix or category as-is
- Example: category="t-deck-plus" → root="t-deck" (if t-deck-plus unknown but t-deck known)
- Related devices: all devices starting with "t-deck-" or "t-deck_"
- Example: "t-deck" family = {t-deck, t-deck-tft, t-deck-tft-ru, t-deck-plus, t-deck-ru, ...}

## OTA Update Flow (Client → Proxy → Upstream)
1. ESP32 device/app calls: GET /firmwares?category=<device_name>
2. Receives list of available versions with fid
3. Selects version → GET /firmwares?fid=<fid>
4. Receives partition info + file URL
5. Verifies partition layout (nb, s, f, ao, as, etc.)
6. Calls: GET /download?fid=<fid>&file=<firmware_url>
7. Streams binary to device for flashing

## Upstream Calls
The proxy makes these upstream calls:
- `GET /srcs` → list of firmware sources [{src, desc, type}]
- `GET /availableFirmwares?src=<src>` → {espdevices, uf2devices, rp2040devices}
- `GET /versions?t=<device>&src=<src>` → {versions: [...], dates: {v: date, ...}}
- `GET /firmware?t=<device>&v=<version>&u=1&src=<src>` → binary (u=1 for OTA, u=5 for ZIP)

## Multi-Source Aggregation
- Queries ALL sources concurrently for device lists (to find devices in third-party repos)
- Aggregates device variants across multiple sources
- Supports per-source filtering in firmware queries
- Example: "t-deck-tft-ru" exists only in "svk" source but is auto-discovered

## Caching Strategy
- Sources: cached after first fetch (in-memory)
- All devices: cached after first fetch (union of all sources)
- No per-version caching (always fresh from upstream)
